steps.observed: Directory exists with bug_report.yml, config.yml, and feature_request.yml

steps.expected: Appropriate bug report template with required fields
steps.observed: Template includes: version, platform, summary, reproduction steps, logs, impact dropdown, and security reference

steps.expected: Appropriate feature request template with required fields
steps.observed: Template includes: problem statement, proposed solution, alternatives, and breaking change risk dropdown

evidence.validation: Both templates are well-structured with appropriate YAML schema, required field validations, and OSS-appropriate guidance

steps.observed: Template includes: type of change checklist (Skill, Fix, Simplification, Release), description section, skill-specific checklist, and release hygiene reminder about CHANGELOG.md

evidence.validation: Template provides clear guidance for PR contributors, distinguishes between skill PRs and source code changes, and reminds about CHANGELOG.md updates

steps.expected: No hardcoded credentials, use GitHub secrets syntax
steps.observed: Uses ${{ secrets.VAR }} syntax where needed, no inline credentials found. All external tools (gitleaks) use official GitHub Actions.

steps.expected: No hardcoded credentials, use GitHub secrets syntax
steps.observed: Uses GitHub Actions context syntax, no hardcoded API keys or tokens. Uses standard permissions (contents: read, issues: write, pull-requests: write).

evidence.validation: Both workflows use GitHub secrets syntax ${{ ... }}, no hardcoded credentials detected, all external dependencies use official GitHub Actions, workflows are declarative and public-suitable

steps.action: Check hooks/ directory exists in repository
steps.observed: hooks/ directory exists at repository root with pre-commit, pre-merge-commit, pre-push files

steps.action: Verify required hook files are present
steps.observed: All three required hooks exist and are executable bash scripts

steps.action: Confirm hooks are tracked by git
steps.expected: Hooks show up in git ls-files
steps.observed: git ls-files hooks/ returned all three hooks, confirming they are tracked

evidence.gitTracked: git ls-files hooks/ returned: hooks/pre-commit, hooks/pre-merge-commit, hooks/pre-push
evidence.validation: All three hooks exist, are executable, are tracked by git, are not ignored, and provide appropriate validation (typecheck, tests, secret-scan)

summary: All 4 governance assertions passed: VAL-GOV-001 (issue templates), VAL-GOV-002 (PR template), VAL-GOV-003 (GitHub Actions OSS-appropriate), VAL-GOV-004 (git hooks tracked). The repository has comprehensive OSS governance with well-structured templates, clean workflows without hardcoded secrets, and tracked git hooks for contributor validation.
